author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU)
content:
MICU DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — UPMC
Date: 5/12/2027 (ICU day ~10)

SUBJECTIVE/EVENTS: Persistent high fevers (Tmax 102-103 F) continue despite ~8 days of vancomycin + meropenem. Hemodynamics worsening — norepinephrine up-titrated; vasopressin likely to be added. Remains unresponsive off sedation.

WORKUP RESULTING:
- Blood cultures (multiple sets since 5/4): NO GROWTH.
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (IMG003): NO abscess or drainable collection; patent TIPS; no source identified.
- Repeat paracentesis (PROC005): negative for SBP.
- Lumbar puncture (PROC006): normal CSF.
- Procalcitonin not elevated; WBC not rising; mild peripheral eosinophilia noted (7%).

OBJECTIVE: Febrile, on pressors, intubated. No localizing exam findings. Lines exchanged/evaluated without clear source.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Persistent fevers + refractory shock — presumed occult infection not yet localized. CONTINUE vancomycin + meropenem; consider adding empiric antifungal coverage; pursue further source workup (repeat imaging, consider TEE for endocarditis, ID consult). Continue pressors.
#Worsening organ function — MELD now ~40 (rising bilirubin/INR/creatinine).
#Mental status — persistent; attributed to hepatic encephalopathy / critical illness.
(Working frame remains occult infection; antibiotics continued and coverage broadened.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Lena Park, MD (MICU). 5/12/2027.
